title: coriandrum setifolium
intro: Coriandrum setifolium, also known as wild fennel or broom coriander, is a perennial herb belonging to the carrot family Apiaceae. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its carminative and antispasmodic effects. The plant's health benefits include digestive relief, anti-inflammatory properties, and its ability to aid in respiratory issues. Coriandrum setifolium can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in culinary dishes for its anise-like flavor.
